sections.paragraph: m., most powerful, Almighty, an epith. of Jehovah; sometimes with אֵל prefixed, Gen 17:1 28:3 Exo 6:3 sometimes simply, Job 5:17 6:4 8:3 13:3 and often in that book; Gen 49:25 Rth 1:20, 21 , etc. It is the plur. excellentiæ, from the sing. שַׁד powerful (compare Arab. شَدِيدُ powerful, strong; root שָׁדַד No. 1); with the plur. termination (Lehrg. p. 523). This has indeed been called in question by Verbrugg. (de Nominum Hebræorum, pl. Numero, ed. 2, Erlang. 1752) and Ewald (Hebr. Gram., p. 298, 423); but without good grounds. LXX. often παντοκράτωρ. Vulg. in Pentat. Omnipotens.
